data.title: Taiwan Tourism Administration Highlights Taiwan's Growing Importance as Asia's Cruise Gateway at Seatrade Cruise Global 2026
data.description: In Miami on April 14, 2026, the Taiwan Tourism Administration (TTA) showcased Taiwan as Asia's prominent cruise gateway at Seatrade Cruise Global 2026. Emphasizing its status under the international tourism brand "Taiwan – Waves of Wonder," TTA positioned Taiwan as a vital hub for the cruise industry.

data.title: Reach Surgical Receives CE Mark Approval for iREACH IRIS, First Powered Reusable Stapler with Real-Time Firing Curve™ and 90° Articulation
data.description: Reach Surgical, the surgical solutions division of Genesis MedTech, has recently been granted CE Mark approval for iREACH IRIS, a state-of-the-art powered tool that offers real-time, location-specific insight into tissue behavior along the staple line. This development was announced in Singapore on April 15, 2026. The approval marks a significant advancement in surgical technology, providing essential information for surgeons during procedures. The iREACH IRIS is designed to enhance precision with advanced features and capabilities. This innovative tool is poised to revolutionize surgical practices with its ability to deliver critical insights at crucial moments, ensuring improved outcomes for patients.

data.title: Constellation Software’s Vela Operating Group Enters into Agreement to acquire a majority interest in Derbysoft Holdings Limited
data.description: On April 14, 2026, Constellation Software Inc. revealed that Juniper Group, an entity under Vela Software, has finalized a deal to purchase the majority stake in Derbysoft Holdings Limited, the parent company of DerbySoft Inc. This agreement marks a significant move in the industry, with Constellation Software's stock trading under the symbol TSX:CSU. The details of the transaction and the exact percentage of the interest acquired were undisclosed in the announcement.

data.title: Metropolitan Board Adopts Two-Year Budget, Commits to Addressing Aging Infrastructure, Future Reliability
data.description: In Los Angeles, the Metropolitan Water District’s Board of Directors has approved a two-year budget aimed at maintaining infrastructure and progressing a new recycled water project. The budget totals $2.3 billion for 2026/27 and $2.4 billion for 2027/28, with a 6.2% overall rate increase effective from Jan. 1. This decision was made unanimously to ensure the continued provision of safe water to communities in Southern California.

data.title: CCTP by Circle enables pay-first settlement for USDC cross-chain payouts, letting platforms delegate payments and settle reimbursements later.
data.description: Circle recently introduced a USDC payment system that operates via fulfillers, enabling platforms to delegate payouts. This new architecture utilizes CCTP and allows for reimbursements to be settled cross-chain at a later time. The innovative approach streamlines processes by assigning payment responsibilities to fulfillers, enhancing efficiency within the system.
